top5.title: Middle East Conflict Escalation Risk
top5.reach: Multiple sources reporting ongoing tensions despite ceasefire.
top5.the_fear: Escalating military conflict between U.S./Israel and Iran will trigger regional warfare, oil supply collapse, and economic crisis.
top5.why_contested: Intelligence officials disagree on Iran's threat level; some say it poses immediate danger while others argue it has been sufficiently degraded. Ceasefire terms remain disputed between parties.
top5.case_for: Oil prices spike $106+, fertilizer costs rise 20%, consumer confidence at record lows; recent military strikes killed thousands.
top5.case_against: Ceasefire being maintained through diplomacy; grain inventories at record highs; some assess Iranian military significantly weakened by prior operations.
questions: Will the ceasefire hold beyond April 26?
How severely has Iran's military capability actually been degraded?
Can economic impacts be contained if fighting resumes?

top5.title: Strait of Hormuz Supply Disruption
top5.reach: Reported daily in energy and economic coverage.
top5.the_fear: Iranian restrictions on shipping through Hormuz will trigger global energy shortage, food crisis via fertilizer costs, and economic shock.
top5.why_contested: Markets are pricing in disruption risk rather than actual blockade; some analysts expect normalization while others predict sustained scarcity.
top5.case_for: Fertilizer prices expected 20% higher if crisis persists; 20%+ of global oil flows through strait; shipping already reduced to trickle.
top5.case_against: FAO estimates record cereal stocks; some ships transiting despite restrictions; analysts warn input shocks take time to fully transmit.
questions: Will Iran maintain toll collection or attempt full closure?
How long can inventory buffers protect food security?
What's the true probability of permanent disruption?

top5.title: Immigration and Public Safety Balance
top5.reach: Actively debated in policy circles and news coverage.
top5.the_fear: Weak enforcement of immigration law and sanctuary city policies enable serious crimes and endanger citizens.
top5.why_contested: Experts split on whether enforcement-focused or root-cause approaches better prevent crime; political polarization shapes perception of threat level.
top5.case_for: Incidents involving immigrants in sanctuary jurisdictions; TSA staffing concerns; enforcement advocates argue strict border control essential.
top5.case_against: Crime causation is multifactorial; enforcement alone doesn't address root causes; many sanctuary-city crime rates remain low.
questions: Do sanctuary policies measurably increase crime risk?
What's the optimal balance of enforcement and integration?
How much does public perception exceed actual threat?

top5.title: AI and Digital Surveillance Threats
top5.reach: Emerging debate in tech policy and security communities.
top5.the_fear: AI systems and expanded government surveillance capabilities will erode privacy, enable authoritarian control, and undermine democratic oversight.
top5.why_contested: Supporters argue modernized tools are essential for national security; critics contend insufficient privacy safeguards and constitutional oversight exist.
top5.case_for: AI decision-making timelines compress; disinformation campaigns amplify; Large Language Models shift human-machine balance toward automation.
top5.case_against: Privacy protections and legislative frameworks exist in some jurisdictions; security agencies claim robust safeguards; threat assessment politicized.
questions: Are privacy protections adequate for AI-driven systems?
Can oversight mechanisms keep pace with technology?
How do security needs balance individual rights?

top5.title: Energy Inflation and Stagflation Risk
top5.reach: Central to consumer sentiment and economic policy discussion.
top5.the_fear: Sustained oil prices above $100/barrel will trigger runaway inflation, crush consumer spending, and derail economic growth.
top5.why_contested: Some economists see temporary shock from geopolitical risk; others worry persistent disruption threatens structural price stability and employment.
top5.case_for: March inflation jumped to 3.3%; consumer sentiment at record low; airlines cutting earnings forecasts due to fuel costs.
top5.case_against: Inflation expectations moderate if ceasefire holds; energy represents subset of economy; monetary policy tools available to manage demand.
questions: Is current inflation transitory or structural?
How much does geopolitical risk premium persist?
Can central banks manage dual mandate if stagflation emerges?

honorable_mentions.title: Ukraine-Russia Negotiation Outcome
honorable_mentions.summary: Experts divided on whether proposed settlements threaten Western security interests or represent pragmatic path to reducing casualties.

honorable_mentions.title: China-Taiwan Cross-Strait Tensions
honorable_mentions.summary: Disagreement over whether U.S. strategic reassurance increases stability or provokes miscalculation in the Taiwan Strait.

honorable_mentions.title: AI Data Center Power Consumption
honorable_mentions.summary: Contested debate on whether rapid energy demands from AI infrastructure justify regulatory intervention or will self-correct through market forces.

honorable_mentions.title: Teen Social Media Restrictions
honorable_mentions.summary: Policy divisions over whether age-based bans protect youth mental health or infringe on expression and parental rights.

honorable_mentions.title: Criminal Justice and Bail Reform
honorable_mentions.summary: Ongoing dispute between those prioritizing public safety through detention and those emphasizing presumption of innocence and rehabilitation.
